mnemonics: QPP: Quality Prevents Problems (Plan for it early in the Project)
keyPoints: Quality is not an afterthought; it must be integrated into the initial project plan and budget.
Planning for quality involves defining standards, processes, and metrics (like defect density) from the start.
Proactive quality planning (e.g., reviews, test planning) reduces long-term costs compared to reactive bug-fixing.
commonMistakes: Treating quality assurance as a separate final phase instead of a continuous activity integrated throughout the project lifecycle.
Assuming that adding more testing at the end can compensate for a lack of quality focus during requirements and design.
